hook_text_english: The godfather of AI just said something I wish I hadn't read.
full_text_english: I don't know how to not think about this anymore.the godfather of AI just said something I wish I hadn't read.Geoffrey Hinton isn't saying AI will take your job. He's saying AI might take jobs as a concept.For 10,000 years, every tech revolution followed the same script. A machine killed one type of work, a new type appeared somewhere else.Tractors killed farm jobs. Factories opened. Factories closed. Offices opened. Offices got automated. Knowledge work opened.Every time one door closed, another one opened behind it. That's the only reason the economy kept working.Hinton's point is that AI isn't opening a new door. It's already standing behind every door you could possibly run to.Call center worker loses their job. Retrains as a coder. AI replaces the coder. Pivots to law. AI replaces law. Tries writing. AI replaces writing.There's no lane left to switch into.Goldman Sachs already estimates two-thirds of jobs in the US and Europe are exposed to AI. Entry-level hiring in AI-exposed fields is down 13% since ChatGPT launched. Job openings overall are down 30%.Humans spent 10,000 years breaking through limitations. Food scarcity. Distance. Physical labor. Every time we hit a ceiling, a bigger economy appeared above it.Hinton is saying human intelligence was the last ceiling. And maybe he's right.But here's what's also true. Every person reading this has access to tools today that didn't exist 3 years ago.The gap between someone who uses AI well and someone who doesn't is now wider than any degree ever was.That gap is the new door.Walk through it.vc: Milk Road AI
body_summary_english: Geoffrey Hinton suggests AI might eliminate jobs as a concept, breaking the historical pattern where new job types replace old ones. This challenges the assumption that economic recovery will follow previous tech revolutions.
comments: If you ask any AI—whether it’s ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok, or Perplexity AI—who talks the most about AI replacing jobs, the answer often points to none other than Vaibhav Sisinty.@ Vaibhav Sisinty, have you heard of the Jevons Paradox? Understanding it might give a different perspective and help reduce unnecessary fear around AI and jobs.

As a BCA student, this hits differently. I'm literally studying to enter the same job market Hinton is talking about. But I've stopped seeing AI as competition and started treating it as the skill gap itself. The students who graduate knowing how to build with AI won't just survive this shift — they'll define it.
The takeaway isn’t job loss—it’s capability shift. The edge now comes from how well you leverage AI, not what role you hold.

Powerful perspective by Vaibhav Sisinty🤯The fear isn’t completely wrong—but it’s incomplete. AI might compress roles, but it also expands leverage. One person today can do what a team of 5–10 used to do.The real shift isn’t “jobs disappearing,” it’s “average skill becoming obsolete.” People who think, adapt, and use AI strategically will still stay valuable.The game is no longer about what you know, it’s about how fast you can learn and apply. 🚀
While past revolutions created new physical or knowledge sectors, this one demands an entirely new baseline of technological fluency. Embracing these tools is no longer optional—it's survival.
The last line about the gap is closer to what’s visible day to day. Teams aren’t replacing everyone, they’re relying heavily on the few who can integrate these tools into actual workflows.
There’s fear in this, but also clarity. Vaibhav Sisinty Maybe the shift isn’t about finding a new lane anymore—it’s about learning how to run faster with AI in every lane.
Important perspective. AI may compress roles, but it expands leverage.The shift isn’t finding a new lane. It’s learning how to operate at a completely different level.
The ability to hold attention has become one of the most valuable skills today. Content that stays with people creates far more impact than content that simply gets views.
Vaibhav Sisinty Powerful perspective. AI may compress traditional job pathways, but it’s also expanding leverage for those who learn to use it effectively.
Every new update from OpenAI, Anthropic or Google gives me the gut feeling that another profession is going to go.

hook_text_english: Elon and I are basically best friends now, I guess
full_text_english: Elon and I are basically best friends now, I guess 🤣Okay not really. But something happened yesterday that's worth talking about.I was on an important call.My phone wouldn't stop buzzing. WhatsApp, Slack all going off at the same time.I genuinely thought something had gone wrong. Server down. Family emergency. Something serious.I opened it and realised what had actually happened:Elon had retweeted my tweet.Half my group chats thought I'd died. The other half thought I'd finally made it.But the retweet isn't the interesting part.The interesting part is what it revealed about how the internet actually works in 2026.One retweet from the right account outperforms a month of consistent posting. Not by a little. By an order of magnitude.That's the game now.And here's what nobody wants to admit:Twitter keeps getting called toxic. Over and over. People keep saying it's dying.But there is no other platform on earth where every top founder, CEO, VC, and world leader is actively doom scrolling at the same time.Not LinkedIn. Not Instagram. Not even close.One post. One reshare from the right person. And suddenly you're in front of everyone who matters.No wonder Elon paid $44 billion to buy it.He wasn't buying a social network.He was buying the last room on the internet where real power still hangs out.

The “$44 billion for a doom-scrolling room” framing is genuinely the best way I’ve heard it explained. Everyone’s been asking why — and the answer has always been hiding in plain sight. CEOs, VCs, founders, world leaders — no other platform has that density of decision-makers in one feed. LinkedIn is résumés. Instagram is aesthetics. X is where actual leverage lives. Wild that a retweet can do what a month of content can’t. Proof that on X, access beats algorithm every time
I agree Twitter has that room, but I’ve also seen serious business happen on LinkedIn. It’s less loud, but more conversion-driven.

transcript: The richest people on earth are trapped. They know AI layoffs will crash the economy. They cannot stop firing workers anyway. Here is why. Welcome to day 22 of AI update of the day. Two researchers just proved AI layoffs will crash the entire economy and every CEO already knows it. The paper is called the AI layoff trap and it is already happening. Oracle fired 12,000 people in India. One email, 6:00 in the morning, job gone. Amazon, Meta, Atlassian, Block, all announced layoffs this year. All blamed AI. Here is the flowchart. Every company fires workers to cut costs. Every fired worker stops buying products. Revenue collapses across every sector. The companies that fired everyone go bankrupt. It is a prisoner's dilemma. And the math is insane. Two companies sell the same thing. Company A keeps its workers. Company B fires them and uses AI. Company B wins on price. Company A has to fire its people just to survive. Now multiply this by every industry on Earth. And this is the part that should scare you. It is not just workers who lose, the owners lose, too. The researchers proved even if you only cared about making rich people richer, this system still destroys more money than it creates. Every CEO can see the cliff. Every CEO keeps driving toward it because the first one to stop dies first. The AI trap on the economy is here. I share updates like these in my WhatsApp community. Link in bio.

OpenAI just dropped the biggest Codex update of the year that might have made Claude Code completely old news. And Anthropic fired back with three major launches including Claude Design, a tool that literally replaces Canva and Figma. Plus 12 more AI updates from this week, and a full Claude Design tutorial at the end of this video.

This week we cover:
• Claude Design - the new Anthropic tool that builds PPTs, websites, apps and full brand videos from a single prompt
• Claude Opus 4.7 + Routines - Anthropic’s model upgrade that runs tasks on its own servers while your laptop is closed
• OpenAI Codex Update - Codex can now open apps on your Mac, find bugs, and fix them without you touching anything

• Google NEET Prep - Gemini now generates completely free NEET UG mock tests for 20 lakh Indian students
• Google Flow Voice - 30 new AI character voices you can lock to any video across every scene
• Perplexity Personal Computer - an AI assistant that runs on your Mac 24/7 and works while you sleep

• YouTube Reimagine - puts you inside any YouTube Short with an 8-second AI video
• AI Parenting (Jesse Genet) - how one mom is raising four kids entirely with AI agents
• Google Colab Learn Mode - the first AI tool designed to make you smarter, not faster
• Google Skills on Chrome - save AI prompts once, reuse them with one click on any webpage
• Zuckerberg AI Clone - Mark Zuckerberg is building a photorealistic AI version of himself for Meta employees

title: OpenAI Just Quietly Took Claude's Spot As AI's King (+14 Updates)
caption:

OpenAI just dropped the biggest Codex update of the year that might have made Claude Code completely old news. And Anthropic fired back with three major launches including Claude Design, a tool that literally replaces Canva and Figma. Plus 12 more AI updates from this week, and a full Claude Design tutorial at the end of this video.

CHAPTERS

This week we cover:
• Claude Design - the new Anthropic tool that builds PPTs, websites, apps and full brand videos from a single prompt
• Claude Opus 4.7 + Routines - Anthropic’s model upgrade that runs tasks on its own servers while your laptop is closed
• OpenAI Codex Update - Codex can now open apps on your Mac, find bugs, and fix them without you touching anything
• GPT-Rosalind - OpenAI’s new drug discovery AI, named after Rosalind Franklin, only released to US pharma companies
• Google NEET Prep - Gemini now generates completely free NEET UG mock tests for 20 lakh Indian students
• Google Flow Voice - 30 new AI character voices you can lock to any video across every scene
• Perplexity Personal Computer - an AI assistant that runs on your Mac 24/7 and works while you sleep
• Google Flow Music - create original music tracks from a text prompt using Lyria 3 Pro
• YouTube Reimagine - puts you inside any YouTube Short with an 8-second AI video
• AI Parenting (Jesse Genet) - how one mom is raising four kids entirely with AI agents
• Google Colab Learn Mode - the first AI tool designed to make you smarter, not faster
• Google Skills on Chrome - save AI prompts once, reuse them with one click on any webpage
• Zuckerberg AI Clone - Mark Zuckerberg is building a photorealistic AI version of himself for Meta employees
• Google Stitch Update - custom logos + copy-paste across projects in under 30 seconds
• Claude Design Tutorial - I build three projects (brand video, pitch deck, landing page) in under 20 minutes at the end

transcript: Sam Alman just dropped an AI that can literally use your computer for you. And Anthropic is quietly becoming the next Apple of AI. Same playbook. Once you are in, you never need to leave. This week alone, they launched three major updates, including a tool that might just replace Canva entirely. And this thing is so powerful, it could threaten the entire design industry and every office job built around making PPTs, websites, or apps. Meanwhile, Google was quietly cooking up the biggest week of all. They came for Hani Singh and every music producer in the country. They came for every coaching institute in India teaching J and Neat. And they built an AI that literally puts you inside any YouTube video you watch. Talk about main character energy. Mark Zuckerberg is quietly building an AI clone of himself to take meetings when he cannot show up. And Perplexity just launched an AI that works entirely while you sleep. And that is just five of the 14 AI updates from this week. Hi, I am Vibhav. I run three companies on AI and train people on AI in over 150 countries. Every week I cover the AI updates from around the world that are actually worth your time. Plus, at the end, I will show you how I used Clawude Design to turn a simple screenshot into a full working landing page, a motion graphics brand video, and a full pitch deck without ever opening PowerPoint. While I make this video once a week, if you want AI updates in real time, I have a WhatsApp community which is free to join and I drop AI updates in time there. Link is in the description. Let us go. Anthropic just launched Claude Design and this is not a mockup tool. Watch what it does. You describe what you want, attach your files and Claude builds the full design in front of you. In this demo, someone typed, "Design an interactive globe showing how culture flows between cities, attached a spreadsheet and a document, and Claude built a full dark themed 3D rotating globe with glowing paths and live statistics. Then they typed add controls and sliders appeared that change the colors in real time. It works for everything. App prototypes, presentation slides, landing pages, dashboards. You can tweak fonts and colors with sliders. Leave comments on specific parts of the design and toggle dark mode all through conversation. And when the design is done, you export it as a PDF, a PowerPoint. Send it straight to Canva or hand it off to Claude code to make it real. Available now on all paid Claude plans. So, Anthropic just dropped a Claude Opus 4.7, a proper upgrade over the Claude 4.6 you have been using. And two things are genuinely different. First, Opus 4.7 sees images in three times higher resolution than 4.6. So, when you upload a screenshot, a photo, or a messy document. It catches the small stuff the old version used to miss. On Anthropic's own testing, image accuracy jumped from 57 to 79%. Second, it is way better at handling multi-step tasks, the kind where you ask Claude to do one thing after another in sequence. So if you tell Claude to research a topic, turn the findings into a table and share it. 4.7 finishes the whole thing with one/ird the errors 4.6 used to make. And it is live right now across every paid Claude plan. No upgrade required. But honestly, the most interesting launch from Anthropic this week isn't the model itself. Claude just launched something called routines. You give it a task once and Claude runs it on its own servers on your schedule even while your laptop is closed. Basically, Claude automates work for you while you are away. We actually made a full 15minute tutorial breaking down exactly how routines work and how to set them up. So, if you want the complete guide, the video is linked here. OpenAI just dropped the biggest Codeex update of the year. And this is one update that isn't just for developers. For anyone new to this, Codeex is OpenAI's AI work assistant that lives inside a desktop app. Up until now, it was mostly used by coders for writing

comments: If you ask any AI—whether it’s ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok, or Perplexity AI—who talks the most about AI replacing jobs, the answer often points to none other than Vaibhav Sisinty.@ Vaibhav Sisinty, have you heard of the Jevons Paradox? Understanding it might give a different perspective and help reduce unnecessary fear around AI and jobs.

As a BCA student, this hits differently. I'm literally studying to enter the same job market Hinton is talking about. But I've stopped seeing AI as competition and started treating it as the skill gap itself. The students who graduate knowing how to build with AI won't just survive this shift — they'll define it.
The takeaway isn’t job loss—it’s capability shift. The edge now comes from how well you leverage AI, not what role you hold.

Powerful perspective by Vaibhav Sisinty🤯The fear isn’t completely wrong—but it’s incomplete. AI might compress roles, but it also expands leverage. One person today can do what a team of 5–10 used to do.The real shift isn’t “jobs disappearing,” it’s “average skill becoming obsolete.” People who think, adapt, and use AI strategically will still stay valuable.The game is no longer about what you know, it’s about how fast you can learn and apply. 🚀
While past revolutions created new physical or knowledge sectors, this one demands an entirely new baseline of technological fluency. Embracing these tools is no longer optional—it's survival.
The last line about the gap is closer to what’s visible day to day. Teams aren’t replacing everyone, they’re relying heavily on the few who can integrate these tools into actual workflows.
There’s fear in this, but also clarity. Vaibhav Sisinty Maybe the shift isn’t about finding a new lane anymore—it’s about learning how to run faster with AI in every lane.
